entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum L., the legume crop studied for salt stress responses

entities.name: SALINITY
entities.description: High salt concentration in soil, specifically NaCl, causing stress to plants

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The total seed production of the chickpea plant

entities.name: ION TOXICITY
entities.description: The harmful effect caused by the accumulation of excessive ions in plant tissues

entities.name: SALT STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea genotypes to maintain productivity under saline conditions

entities.name: SALT STRESS SENSITIVITY
entities.description: The susceptibility of chickpea genotypes to yield reduction under saline conditions

entities.name: OUTDOOR POT EXPERIMENT
entities.description: The experimental setup used to grow chickpea genotypes in treated soil

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: The phenological stage of flower development in chickpea

entities.name: FILLED POD NUMBER
entities.description: The number of pods containing developed seeds

entities.name: SEED NUMBER
entities.description: The total count of seeds produced per plant

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: The physical dimensions of the produced seeds

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE SUCCESS
entities.description: The overall efficiency of the plant in producing viable seeds

entities.name: K+ CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The concentration of potassium ions in plant tissues, specifically seeds

entities.name: NA+ CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The concentration of sodium ions in plant tissues, specifically leaves

entities.name: CL- CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The concentration of chloride ions in plant tissues, specifically mature seeds

entities.name: K+/NA+ RATIO
entities.description: The ratio of potassium to sodium ions in the laminae of young leaves

relationships.description: reduces yields in several regions of the world
impairs reproductive success more in sensitive lines
higher concentrations in leaves are associated with delayed flowering
accumulation in leaves is associated with lower reproductive success via delayed flowering
higher concentration in seeds at mid-filling stage is positively correlated with higher seed yields
higher ratio in laminae of young leaves is positively correlated with higher seed yields
lower concentration in old green leaves is positively correlated with higher seed yields
higher concentration in mature seeds is positively correlated with higher seed yields
associated with seed yield in saline conditions
associated with seed yield in saline conditions
not associated with seed yield in saline conditions
used to study yield parameters and ion analysis of chickpea genotypes
associated with ion toxicity leading to yield reductions
associated with phenological and yield parameters
associated with phenological and yield parameters
